title: A 'valid' email can score low — the 0-100 score penalizes catch-all / disposable / role
summary: SpiderVerify returns each email with a status (valid/invalid/risky/unknown) AND a 0-100 score. The score starts at 50 and penalizes catch-all (-15), disposable (-30), and role accounts (-10), so a syntactically valid, MX-present, deliverable address on a catch-all domain lands in the 60s and a valid disposable can score ~30. status:valid + a mid score is not a contradiction — explain it from the quality flags (is_catch_all / is_disposable / is_role_account), not by calling the email 'bad'. A catch-all domain in particular caps confidence because the server accepts every address, so SMTP can't prove the specific mailbox exists.
